mission.title: kubeedge: [lfx-mentorship] Enhance KubeEdge testing coverage
mission.description: [lfx-mentorship] Enhance KubeEdge testing coverage. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current kubeedge deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kubeedge version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubeedge -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeedge
helm list -n kubeedge 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kubeedge installation.

steps.title: Review kubeedge configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubeedge configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubeedge -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeedge
kubectl get configmap -n kubeedge -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubeedge
```
**What would you like to be added/modified**:

KubeEdge would like to improve the UT coverage of the code to better maintain the quality of the code and reduce the introduction of defects.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [lfx-mentorship] Enhance KubeEdge testing coverage
steps.description:
Unit test for edge's sub-directory
Part of [lfx-mentorship] Enhance KubeEdge testing coverage initiative https://github.com/kubeedge/kubeedge/issues/6101
NONE

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubeedge -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeedge
kubectl get events -n kubeedge --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[lfx-mentorship] Enhance KubeEdge testing coverage" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Unit test for edge's sub-directory
Part of [lfx-mentorship] Enhance KubeEdge testing coverage initiative https://github.com/kubeedge/kubeedge/issues/6101
NONE

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubeedge installed or the issue environment reproducible.
